summary: Adjusted the Makefile compiler/version selection so lbtime could compile/match cleanly, then replaced the hand-written asm fallback for func_8000AFBC with the C implementation. The PR body only says "lbtime OKs" and there were no review comments, so the inferred intent is a build/matching fix for lbtime driven by compiler configuration.
what_changed: Changed the default MWCC_VERSION in the Makefile from 1.1 to 1.2.5.
Kept EPILOGUE_PROCESS using MWCC_EPI_VERSION 1.2.5e, with assignment/operator formatting adjusted for MWCC_EPI_VERSION and CC_EPI.
Added a target-specific override so $(EPILOGUE_DIR)/src/melee/lb/lbtime.o uses CC_EPI := $(CC) instead of overriding MWCC_EPI_VERSION to 1.2.5.
Removed the inline asm implementation of func_8000AFBC from src/melee/lb/lbtime.c.
Restored the C implementation of func_8000AFBC, which computes OSGetTime divided by the timebase value at 0x800000F8 / 4, clamps above UINT_MAX, and returns the u32 result.
smart_moves: Solved the lbtime issue at the build-configuration level instead of preserving a local asm workaround.
Used a target-specific Makefile override for lbtime.o, keeping the special compiler behavior scoped to the one object that needed it.
Removed a large inline asm block once the compiler setup made the concise C version acceptable, improving readability and future maintainability.
Preserved EPILOGUE_PROCESS-specific compiler handling rather than flattening all compiler versions into one global path.
decomp_lessons: When a function only fails due to scheduling or compiler-version behavior, test whether a per-object compiler/tool selection can make the C source match before resorting to inline asm.
Target-specific Makefile variables are useful for decompilation projects where individual objects need different MWCC behavior.
Inline asm fallbacks should be treated as temporary when the equivalent C is known and can be made to match through build settings.
Compiler and linker MWCC versions may need to differ; matching one object can depend on both compile and link tool choices.
naming_conventions: The function names remained placeholder-style decomp names: func_8000AFBC and func_8000B028.
Makefile variables used uppercase tool/version names such as MWCC_VERSION, MWCC_EPI_VERSION, MWCC_LD_VERSION, CC_EPI, and EPILOGUE_DIR.
No new semantic symbol names were introduced in this PR.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removed an asm u32 func_8000AFBC implementation marked as a nonmatching workaround due to scheduling silliness.
Restored the C version of func_8000AFBC after changing compiler selection.
Used a per-object Makefile override for lbtime.o: CC_EPI := $(CC).
The removed asm called OSGetTime and __div2i, then clamped to UINT_MAX; the retained C expresses the same logic directly.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename func_8000AFBC or document lbtime semantics.
Search for other target-specific Makefile compiler overrides in EPILOGUE_DIR objects.
Look for PRs that replaced inline asm with C after MWCC version changes.
Check whether MWCC_VERSION 1.2.5 and MWCC_LD_VERSION 1.1 remained the stable project defaults after this PR.
